MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Or Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-27-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joel Zeldin and Roey Rahmil, counsel for the Estate of William Coblentz (errors and omissions insurer); Jess R. Booth (appearance by Courtcall), counsel for the Estate of William Coblentz and the Estate of Ellery McClatchy (directors and officers insurer); Gilmur R. Murray and Mark L. Mosley, counsel for Carlos McClatchy.

At the outset, the Court confirms with Mr. Murray and Mr. Mosley that they are not contesting the Court's tentative ruling on the Carlos McClatchy Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings which they confirm. The Court confirms the tentative ruling and denies the Carlos McClatchy Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. The prevailing party is to prepare the proposed order.

The Court invites Mr. Zeldin to address the Court's tentative ruling with respect to the Motion for Summary Adjudication/Summary Judgment. Mr. Zeldin addresses the relation-back issue with respect to the Probate Code statute of limitations and thereafter addresses the statute of limitations issue with respect to CCP Sec. 340.6 (legal malpractice) and the issue as to whether the late Mr. Coblentz's responsibilities as "trustee" were ancillary, non-legal services related to his law practice. Mr. Murray responds. Mr. Rohmil replies. The matter is submitted.

The Court finds that the 2014 amendments relate back to 2012 petition and as such, are within the Probate Code statute of limitation provisions and denies the motion on that basis. However, with respect to the CCP Sec. 340.6 statute of limitations argument, the Court takes the matter under submission. Counsel are to submit proposed Orders reflecting the Court's ruling with respect to the relation-back issue as to the Probate Code statute of limitations but, with respect to the CCP Sec. 340.6 statute of limitations, Mr. Zeldin's proposed Order should reflect a ruling in his client's favor and Mr. Murray should reflect a ruling in his client's favor.

Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Jaime; Reporter: Christine L. Jordan, CSR# 12262, Behmke Reporting and Video Services, 415-597-5600.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Declaration Of Carlos Mcclatchy Regarding Economic Inability To Pay For Discovery Referee Under Seal
Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ryan Cunningham for respondent co-trustees. Sarah Palsa for Carlos McClatchy (petitioner).

Having confirmed with counsel that there is no contest to the tentative rulings, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: The motion to seal is denied. The declaration does not include the kinds of private information normally sealed, such as bank account numbers or details, or tax information. It includes no specifics concerning his real or personal property holdings, some of which is available through public records. It includes only general statements intended to support Petitioner's claim of indigency that he has made repeatedly in the open record. Petitioner has put his financial standing at issue, and his personal desire to shield the general information in this declaration does not outweigh the public's interest in free access to the courts. In any event, the requested sealing is not narrowly tailored to address only whatever specific, private information it is that Petitioner feels should be excluded from public access. Mr. Cunningham provided the court with a form of order that tracks the tentative.

Judge Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Nancy Wong, Bailiff: Deputy Maloney. Not Reported, D501.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Modifying The Pro Rata Shares Of The Cost Of The Discovery Referee Based On Petitioners Economic Hardship
Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ryan Cunningham for repondent co-trustees. Susan Palsa for Carlos McClatchy (peitioner).

Having confirmed with counsel that there is no contest to the tentative ruling, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Denied. Respondent has not carried his burden of showing that he has an economic inablility to pay a pro rata share of the discovery master's fees. The information Respondent has provided to the Court shows that he has significant wealth. Even assuming he has spent the substantial income he has received over the past years, he continues to hold substantial real and personal property assets. His choice to hold his wealth in these forms, while perhaps not the wisest portfolio management strategy under the circumstances, cannot be a basis to conclude that he cannot bear his fair share of litigation expenses. He has the economic ability to do so. Therefore the Court will not modify its prior order in this regard. Mr. Cunningham provides a form of order that tracks the tentative.

Judge Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Malohney. Not Reported, D501

MINI MINUTES:

Ellen McKissock and Ryan Cunningham for Co-Trustees/Respondent.
Rodney Strickland for The McClatcjy Company.
Joel Zeldin for the Estate of William Coblentz.
Bruce Feder Guardian Ad Litem for two Minors.

Discussion was held with regard to trial setting, close of discovery, expert discovery cut off, and whether or not the $41,000.00 payment to the Discovery Referee will be sufficient payment for completion of all of the pending motions. The Court made it clear to the parties that it does not want the Referee to be put in a position where she will only be able to complete partial resolution of the remaining matters due to lack of ensured payment. The parties are to discuss how further payment to the Referee will be accomplished if needed, and whether or not the parties will be willing to do an advance to secure said payment. The Petition for Order Construing Intent of Settlor of the Trust for the Primary Benefit of James McClatchy and the Amended Petition and Complaint for Declaratory Relief, etc. are continued to March 7, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a further status conference. If the parties reach a stipulation, they may present it to the Court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Denied

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment On Petition Of Respondent Cotrustees For Order Construing Intent Of Settlor Of The Trust For The Primary Benefit Of James B. Mcclatchy
Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors, Mark Mosley & Gilmur Murray for (petitioner) Carlos McClatchy who is present in court. Ellen McKissock for the Co-Trustees. Rod Strickland for the McClatchy Company. Joel Zeldin for the Estate of William Coblentz, deceased, re: the Coblentz firm insurance policy.
The Court stated that we are here on 4 pending motions, that in regards to the Motion to Strike it is inclined to deny, that there is no bar to the trust bringing a petition under 17200 to adjudicate the intent, that it is not duplicative of Carlos' petition but not enough to say that they cannot seek adjudication, but the court is not inclined to toss it under 17202. That in regards to the Motion for Summary Adjudication and the Motion for Summary Judgment the trustees ask the court to adjudicate summarily that the intent of the settlor is that stock not be sold so that the company remains in the family, Carlos wants adjudication that the trustees have a duty to generate income, the court is inclined to deny both stating that the trust is more nuanced than either of these extreme petitions, that Eleanor McClatchy created the trust and structured it in a complex way, that the court cannot ignore that she provided the trustees with the discretion to sell together with other provisions in the trust, that the trustees need to make difficult decisions as to what to do with particular situations within the structure of their duty. That in regards to the Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings if it is concluded that the trustees formulation cannot stand as presented it is inclined to deny, that there is still a viable 17200 petition, that if they cannot resolve this amongst each other they are to get ready for an evidentiary hearing or trial. Ms. McKissock stated that they respect the courts decision, that they have no issue regarding taking the petition to an evidentiary hearing and that they are trying to set the intent as of 1974. The Court stated that it seems unlikely that it makes sense to hold a hearing on the trustees petition independent of Carlos' petition and that a lot of what is relevant to on is relevant to the other. Ms. McKissock stated that the trustees are trying to administer this trust along with 2 others, that they have been in this for 3 years and are not past the petition stage, that there is an appeal and a request for a jury trial for a fraud claim and that this particular petition is basic in caselaw. The Court stated that this afternoon is just about the motions. Ms. McKissock stated that in regards to the Motion for Summary Adjudication it states that they are asking for a decision on the petition to construe, that the prayer does say "or whatever relief the court will give", that they have given the court a wealth of information and that the notice of motion requested judgment on the petition. The Court stated but it does not turn it into an open ended hearing and that it understands everyone's position. Mr. Connors stated that in regards to the Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings they are not trying to establish intent and that it is directed at the trustee's prayer for intent. The Court suggested a 17200 request for instructions. Mr. Connors responded that this petition is more specific. The Court stated that his client knows exactly what it is and that both sides will present their best evidence. The Court further stated that the motions are denied for the reasons provided, that the prevailing parties are to prepare forms of order, that they are to talk to each other regarding the orders and to talk to each other before the February 2, 2016 hearings as to how to proceed, that ideally this should all be resolved together, that they should talk to each other as to how to structure the trustee's petition and to think about how the appeals will effect what the court can decide.
Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rodrin. Court Reporter: Lisa Helgoe, CSR #12216, 408-287-7500 (Reported)

The Mcclatchy Co. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Amended And Supplemental Petition And Complaint For Declaratory Relief, Breach Of Trust, Aiding And Abetting Breach Of Trust, And Fraud
Off Calendar Nov-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order

Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer Of The Estate Of William Coblentz, Deceased, To The Amended And Supplemental Petition And Complaint And The First Amendment Thereto; And Notice Of Its Motion To Strike Punitive Damage Allegations (Only Re: The Coblentz Law Firms Lawyers Professional Liability Insurance Policy)
Off Calendar Nov-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joel Zeldin & Roey Rahmil for the Estate of William Coblentz, deceased. Gilmur Murray & Mark Mosely for Carlos McClatchy. Howard Slavitt, General Counsel for the Coblentz Law Firm.

The Court confirmed with counsel that the tentative on the Motion to Quash is confirmed and that the prevailing party is to submit a proposed order. Mr. Zeldin presented oral argument, read excerpts from the tentative regarding non fraud breach of trust and from an opinion regarding time bar claims, cited the Quintilliani case and 340.6 regarding the allegations, that they believe the tentative is mistaken regarding the 7th claim of relief and read to the court regarding such, that it does not meet the test in the Lee case and that if 340.6 does not apply it will never apply to a trustee who is an attorney, cited P.C. 16460, gave a timeline of events leading up to the February 2015 petition against the Estate of William Coblentz, that the statute started running when the accounting was submitted in February of 2010 and ended in February of 2014 and requested that the Court review the Quintilliani case, sustain the demurrer pursuant to 340.6 or on the basis of P.C. 16460 and rule on the objections to Judicial Notice. Mr. Murray responded that the probate code says that a trustee with special skills needs to use those skills, that Mr. Coblentz had the duty of care and loyalty the same as the other trustees, that they have indemnification agreements, that the duty of loyalty claims are to Mr. Coblentz as a trustee not as an attorney, cited cases that have arisen out of attorney client relationships, that the claims they are alleging are as a trustee and breach of duty as a trustee, that the attorney statute of limitations does not apply to him as a trustee and that when you assume a non legal role you assume the statute as such. Mr. Zeldin stated that 340.6 applies, cited the Prakashpalan case and that 340.6 trumps the statute of limitations in the probate code. The Court stated that it is concerned whether there is a claim that can go forward and that they are relying on allegations. Mr. Zeldin stated that the Lee case was a demurrer case, that it got rid of all causes of action but could state one for conversion and that they request the same test be used here. The Court took the Demurrer under submission requesting that Mr. Zeldin submit 3 forms of order, 1 that follows the tentative, 1 that follows the tentative and sustains the 7th cause of action without leave to amend, and 1 that sustains the entire demurrer.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago. Court Reporter Suzanne Andrade, CSR #10682, 415-597-5600 (Reported)

The Court asked about mediation. Mr. Strickland reported that they were unable to come to a resolution at mediation, that the trustees are planning to file a motion and following the outcome of that motion they will meet again. Mr. Mosely confirmed that they are back in full litigation mode. Ms. McKissock stated that they will be filing a Motion for Summary Judgment (MSJ), that they are working with counsel on a mutual date, that the discovery motion has to do with discovery relevant to the cut off avenues and that there were no facts in the discovery responses. Mr. Mosely stated that the case will end up being tried. The Court stated that this department will manage the case until it thinks it is ready for trial, that the next meeting will be for a case management plan, that they should be actively getting the case ready for trial and that it should not be enormously complicated. Ms. McKissock stated that the MSJ will give counsel a better idea as to how to settle the case, that they might need an evidentiary hearing on an issue and that they are at a point to set a date for the MSJ. The Court asked if the continuance date should be after the motion. Mr. Strickland stated that they will be taking depositions and that the next date should be after the MSJ. The amended and supplemental petition and complaint for declaratory relief, etc., and the petition for order construing intent of settlor of the trust for the primary benefit of James B. McClatchy were continued to February 2, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the parties have done everything to get back on with a settlement/mediation date. A joint case management plan is to be filed one week prior to the continuance date.

Amended And Supplemental Petition And Complaint For Declaratory Relief, Breach Of Trust, Aiding And Abetting Breach Of Trust, And Fraud
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Continued To Oct-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ellen McKissock & Darren Donovan for (Trustees) Gary Pruitt, Leroy Barnes, Ted Mitchell, William McClatchy & Kevin McClatchy. Rod Strickland for the McClatchy Company. Gilmur Murray & Brian Connors for Carlos McClatchy.

The Court stated that we are here on two petitions, that it does not see how this can be decided today, that there is no Motion for Summary Judgment pending, that the matter is contested and that it is inclined to continue to a date past the October 6, 2015 mediation date. Ms. McKissock stated that it would be helpful to have the court's view or to assist the parties in speaking the same language. The Court stated that that is the mediator's function not the court's function and that it would not be appropriate to give its thoughts on the merits. Ms. McKissock stated that the petition was filed in April, that there has been no offer of proof of evidence that can be tried, that interpreting the trust is a judicial function and cited probate code 1046. The Court stated that the petition is premised on interpretation, that there is a mediation date in place and that is a good next step, that if mediation was not set the next step would be to meet and confer and then set an evidentiary hearing and asked if the September 3, 2015 discovery motion needed to go forward with the mediation pending. Ms. McKissock responded that the motion could trail the mediation for the court's convenience. The amended and supplemental petition and complaint for declaratory relief, etc. and the petition of respondent co-trustees for order construing intent of settlor of the trust etc., were continued to October 27, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the matter is not resolved in mediation, the parties are expected to have met and conferred as to how to proceed. The discovery motion is off calendar, subject to being put back on calendar if necessary.
